content.claim: Hungary's Education Minister Judit Lannert in 2026 characterized 6- and 8-year secondary schools as mechanisms for elite self-separation rather than educational development, arguing that student performance gains are primarily explained by family background rather than institutional value-added; she indicated the government would examine possible reforms to the admissions process.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Represents interpretive claim by individual policymaker, not empirical finding
Explicitly contested by opposition (Fidesz parliamentary group leader Gergely Gulyás) on both substantive and process (consultation) grounds
Does not constitute settled policy or consensus; reflects opening position in active political dispute
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
